preamble: The shift from a voiced palatal stop [ɟ] to a voiced alveolar sibilant [z] parallels the voiceless shift, often occurring as part of a systematic palatalization and sibilantization process in Romance languages.

examples.note: Historical development of intervocalic palatal stops into voiced sibilants in Old French.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
